_comment: Structural grounding rules — a *fallback* layer for entity.html when phrase-level grounding (node_phrases.json) produces no matches in a given Archive text. Each entry is keyed by node id and supplies two keyword lists (primary + secondary) plus a hand-written explanation. A sentence in the rendered prose counts as evidence for a node when it contains at least one primary keyword AND at least one secondary keyword (case-insensitive substring). Up to `max_evidence` verbatim sentences are quoted, in document order, and the explanation is shown beneath them. The browser only renders this section when (a) the node contributes to this text via node_to_archive.json AND (b) no phrase grounding spans exist for that node AND (c) at least one evidence sentence is found. Honest absence wins: if no rule matches, nothing is shown — the connection is left to the corpus-locator metadata alone.

CS-fd9b8818.label: discipline-of-mind
CS-fd9b8818.explanation: These lines describe the mind as restless and in need of being trained, guarded, or steadied — the structural pattern of cognitive regulation.

CS-1ad1dc09.label: renunciation-of-world
CS-1ad1dc09.explanation: These lines describe the act of leaving, abandoning, or releasing — the structural pattern of renunciation.

IM-69f621d6.label: self-as-process-of-aggregates
IM-69f621d6.explanation: These lines treat mind or thought as a flow rather than a fixed thing — the structural pattern of self-as-process.

CM-d6d2c618.label: action-bears-fruit
CM-d6d2c618.explanation: These lines describe a quality of action producing a fitting quality of outcome — the structural pattern of moral causation.
